ideas.title: Bring registry design into ts-project-planner too, if ts-project-planner utilizes various skills/plugins
ideas.notes: Seeded from tasks/ideasToImproveOrchestration.md's raw note '# learn ts-have registry design in ts-project-planner as well', refined by user 2026-07-05 to scope it: only relevant if/where ts-project-planner itself calls out to multiple skills/plugins (mirroring ts-deliver-router's CHECKS REGISTRY primitive — DIAL + check-type rows in registry-<phase>.md). Needs /ts-discover explore to confirm ts-project-planner actually has enough skill/plugin fan-out to warrant its own registry before scoping further.

ideas.title: Golden agent-context templates, recommended-to-add by ts-project-init-advisor

riskiest_assumptions.assumption: MODIFY recommendations can meaningfully distinguish missing golden content from intentional user customization when diffing an existing CLAUDE.md/AGENTS.md against the golden template — otherwise recommendations become noise users learn to skip

The five scaffold files (incl. .github/copilot-instructions.md, added 2026-07-04) deserve 'golden template' status — they must be maintained as canonical, kept in parity with this repo's own evolving .claude context conventions, and versioned with releases (upgrades R-03 from tracking-only to a maintenance commitment)
Direct creation when the file is absent is universally safe — no project layout where writing CLAUDE.md/AGENTS.md/.claude/* unprompted is unwelcome (e.g. monorepo subfolder with root-level context)
Advisor path and the installer's existing SCAFFOLD=y blind-copy path can coexist without conflicting or drifting

domain_events: TemplateAbsenceDetected → TemplateCreatedDirectly (greenfield: agent writes golden copy, no approval needed — nothing to clobber)
TemplateDeviationDetected → ModifyRecommendationEmitted (existing file: gap analysis vs golden, approval-gated PROJECT_INIT_PLAN.md task, never auto-overwrite)
aggregates: GoldenTemplateSet (the 4 packaged source files, canonical + versioned per release)
bounded_contexts: template-packaging (exists: manifest + build-release + installer scaffold path)
advisor-planning (exists: PROJECT_INIT_PLAN.md generation — recommendation tasks land here, no new machinery)
exploration_output.acpl_pattern_group: Information Display + Commanded Behaviour (Specification for gap detection, Explicit Empty for no-gaps case, Guard Clause on the absent-only direct-create path; MODIFY writes stay behind the existing approval-gated task execution)

ideas.notes: Original request 2026-07-03: copy-or-merge the four src/project_root_structure/ agent-context files (CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, .claude/CLAUDE.md, .claude/goverance_CLAUDE.md) into the user's project when ts-project-init-advisor runs. PIVOT v2 2026-07-04: golden templates + recommend-to-add only (advisor never writes). PIVOT v3 2026-07-04 (during decide, current scope): golden templates + recommend ADD-or-MODIFY, with a direct-create carve-out — when a target file does NOT exist the agent creates it directly from the golden template (nothing to clobber, no approval friction); when it EXISTS the advisor emits a MODIFY recommendation from gap analysis vs golden as an approval-gated PROJECT_INIT_PLAN.md task, never auto-overwriting. The v2 H-risk dissolution holds (still no automated merge); new top M-risk is distinguishing intentional user customization from missing golden content in MODIFY gap analysis. Current state unchanged: advisor SKILL.md never mentions the scaffold; install.sh SCAFFOLD=y blind-copy path still exists and needs a coexistence posture. Precedent: ts-project-scaffolder's Protected Files Policy. Decision build recorded on v3 scope; validation skipped — no H-risk assumptions. SCOPE ADDENDUM 2026-07-04: golden set expanded to 5 files — .github/copilot-instructions.md added as fifth (user decision, post-sync; plan.json epic notes carry the authoritative file list).

ideas.title: Dual-track orchestration: guidance/routing tables, Discovery sub-agents (ts-event-storming-facilitator, ts-ddd-tactical-validator), WORK_TYPE expansion

riskiest_assumptions.assumption: ts-event-storming-facilitator and ts-ddd-tactical-validator can be shipped as static sub-agent prompt files (no code, no schema enforcement) and still reliably return parseable structured JSON that /ts-discover explore|validate can write into discovery.json without a validation layer catching malformed output

5 new work-type spines (feature/hotfix/chore/patch/spike/ops) plus back-compat 'epic' can stay in lockstep across 4 independent copies (ts-orchestrate SKILL.md, phase-routing.ts, work-unit-profiles.md, hook's duplicated [NEXT] bash strings) without drifting — the runbook accepts this as permanent duplication rather than consolidating
Fixing the pre-existing .ai→.agents test-path breakage in the same change as the dual-track feature won't mask why CI never caught 2 whole test files failing before now

The new manifest.json agents[] category is safe to consume by install.sh, install.ps1, and dogfood.mjs without a schema version bump — every consumer degrades gracefully when the key is absent (old zips), not just the one (install.sh) that was hand-verified
End users can reliably tell SPIKE (Delivery, learning feeds back) apart from POC (Discovery-only, never ships) from the work-type name alone, without hitting the wrong track

aggregates: Idea (discovery.json entry — owns exploration_output/validation_output/decision/riskiest_assumptions; invariants: WIP limit 3, DDD-gate before build)
Epic (iteration.json entry — owns current_phase/gates/work_type; invariant: required gate sign-off blocks phase transition)
ReleaseManifest (scripts/release-manifest.json — owns skills/hooks/agents entries; invariant: always regenerated, never hand-edited)
bounded_contexts: discovery (ts-project-planner Layer D — Idea aggregate, WIP/stale/dedup rules)
delivery (ts-deliver-router — Epic aggregate, phase spine, gates)
packaging (release/build scripts — ReleaseManifest aggregate, agents/skills/hooks categories, installer parity)

orchestration (ts-orchestrate — cross-cutting routing/guidance, reads discovery+delivery state directly via hook rather than through an aggregate API; shared-workspace convention, not an external system, so no ACL — flagged as a coordination point, not a violation)
exploration_output.acpl_pattern_group: Workpiece (Aggregate Root for Idea/Epic invariants + State Machine for idea lifecycle and phase spines + Command Object for routing commands, audited via history.jsonl + Guard Clause on WIP/gate preconditions)

validation_output.council_verdict: H1 (schema-less sub-agent output): PARTIALLY MITIGATED, not resolved. Both agent specs (T1) already define an explicit output contract + a caller-side empty-field gate (T9 step 5: non-empty exploration_output required to exit explore) — but that is an emptiness check, not a schema/type check. A structurally malformed-but-non-empty JSON blob (wrong key names, wrong types) would pass the gate and corrupt discovery.json silently. Recommend: add a mechanical JSON-shape assertion (required keys + array types) alongside the existing non-empty check before any explore/validate write — not a redesign, an additive guard. Does not block build; scope it as a T9 follow-up line item. H2 (4-way spine duplication): CONFIRMED risk, PARTIALLY ADDRESSED. The runbook already reasons about this (§0 D9 'accepted duplication', hook-output tests as the lockstep enforcer) but that only covers hook↔SKILL.md consistency — there is no test asserting phase-routing.ts arrays match SKILL.md's prose spines, or that work-unit-profiles.md profile blocks match either. Recommend one small integration test post-build (parse the 3 sources, assert equal spine arrays per work type) as a follow-up, not a blocker.
validation_output.decision_rationale: Strategic fit HIGH: this closes a real, multi-doc-referenced gap (ts-event-storming-facilitator / ts-ddd-tactical-validator have been 'pending-setup' since the planner PRD, referenced in 4+ docs, never built) and completes architecture this project already committed to (dual-track agile, Layer D/0/1/2 per ts-project-planner/SKILL.md). Not scope creep. Both H-risks are real but addressable with additive guards/tests, not blocking redesigns — feasibility is 'risky' rather than 'infeasible'. tows: Strengths = closes long-standing doc debt, industry-recognizable WORK_TYPE taxonomy. Weaknesses = no schema validation on sub-agent output, no cross-file consistency test. Opportunities = removes 'referenced but unbuilt' confusion for future sessions/models. Threats = large single-change surface (12 tasks, packaging pipeline touched) — Delivery-track risk (installer parity) to watch in Review/Test phases, not a Discovery blocker.

violations.detail: SyncPlan, StartIteration, AdvanceIteration mutate a Release/Iteration concept that isn't modeled as its own aggregate — they currently fold implicitly into the Idea/Epic aggregates. Not a hard violation (legitimate as cross-aggregate process/saga commands) but the model is under-specified.
WorkflowStateInjected is listed as a domain_event but nothing 'happened' to an aggregate — it's a read-side projection (hook output), not a state change. Terminology looseness.

ideas.notes: Seeded from tasks/dual-track-orchestration-plan.md — a Fable 5-authored weak-model-executable runbook (T1-T12) already carrying 12 locked design decisions (see runbook §0). Ran explore via facilitator-role simulation (no ts-event-storming-facilitator sub-agent exists yet — building it IS this idea's T1). 2 H-risk assumptions found → validation was MANDATORY per discovery-kanban.md. Ran validate via council-advisor/tows-strategy-analyst/ts-ddd-tactical-validator role simulation (same reason — none of these sub-agents exist yet either). Result: feasibility=risky, ddd_validation=NEEDS_ATTENTION (not FAIL). DECIDE 2026-07-05: build. Single epic (not split) — the 4 bounded_contexts (discovery/delivery/packaging/orchestration) are one tightly-coupled change: the runbook's own T1-T12 sequence and single Spectra proposal (T11) assume they ship together; SKILL.md's new tables reference agents that only exist after T1, tests in T4/T5/T8 assume phase-routing.ts and hook are edited in the same change. Epic notes must carry forward 3 non-blocking follow-ups from validate: (1) add a JSON-shape/type check for sub-agent output, not just the existing non-empty check; (2) add a test asserting phase-routing.ts spines match SKILL.md/work-unit-profiles.md; (3) DDD model gap — Release/Iteration is under-modeled as its own aggregate, WorkflowStateInjected mis-classified as a domain event rather than a projection. Awaiting /ts-project plan --sync to add EPIC-DUAL-TRACK-ORCHESTRATION to backlog. SHIPPED 2026-07-05: Review→Test→Ship→Reflect completed, pushed origin/master 40d2b85..5f6537b, G2 signed off by ctony. Follow-up (2) fulfilled by T9.5 (spine-consistency.test.ts) but only for phase-routing.ts vs work-unit-profiles.md — Review phase found the broader claim in design.md D9 (hook vs SKILL.md lockstep 'enforced by tests') was false and corrected it; that specific gap remains open. Follow-ups (1) and (3) still open. New follow-up from Review: installer/build-script agents-category wiring is a rule-of-three copy-paste (install.sh/.ps1, build-release.mjs, dogfood.mjs) with no shared helper — refactor-scope, non-blocking. Retro: .agents/ts-project-planner/retrospectives/Iter1-retro.md.

ideas.title: PLDD portable enforcement kernel and consumer adapters

riskiest_assumptions.assumption: A zero-new-dependency Node checker can parse enough TypeScript and JavaScript import forms to provide credible Phase A architecture enforcement.
Codex and Claude Code portability can be proven through identical commands, diagnostics, and fixture outcomes without modifying live host configuration during Phase A or Phase B.
The discovery model can gain explicit command-to-aggregate and event relationships without breaking current consumers of exploration_output.
Stable contract ids remain understandable enough that weak models create and maintain matching violation tests reliably.
Cards, agents, and templates can reference canonical kernel rule ids without duplicating normative rules.
Consumer adapters and installers can be fully tested against fixtures while remaining inactive in agenticToolset until Dogfood Activation approval.

assumptions: Phase A and Phase B modify only files below src.
Existing dual-track topology, router algorithm, state sovereignty, and G1/G2 remain unchanged.
Dogfood activation is a separate human approval after Phase A and Phase B evidence review.
Equipment Lending Board remains parked and outside this discovery idea.

validation_output.council_verdict: DDD model is now internally complete: all 10 commands and 10 events have exactly one aggregate relation, all referenced aggregates exist, Consumer Integration declares Codex, Claude Code, and dependency-cruiser adapter boundaries, and ubiquitous-language coverage is 1.00. Remaining delivery risk is the Phase A parser subset: it must enumerate supported syntax and fail closed on unsupported forms.
validation_output.decision_rationale: Strategic fit remains high. Versioned additive relations preserve legacy arrays while enabling deterministic scenario compilation and DDD validation. Cross-host parity remains testable through portable commands. Proceeding to a build decision is permissible because DDD validation passes, but implementation must retain Phase A/B isolation and the later Dogfood Activation Gate.

ideas.notes: Explored from tasks/harnessPRD/PRD-PLDD-multi-scale-pattern-language.md. Phase A/B isolation and Dogfood Activation boundary are mandatory. VALIDATE 2026-07-13: feasibility=risky; DDD Mode A=FAIL. Build blocked pending explicit command/event ownership, host ACL boundaries, and vocabulary coverage >=0.5; re-run explore then validate. RE-EXPLORE 2026-07-13: added ownership_relations_v1 and external_adapters_v1 without changing legacy array types; aligned ubiquitous language with modeled names. REVALIDATE 2026-07-13: DDD Mode A PASS; ownership 10/10 commands and 10/10 events; 3 external adapters declared; ubiquitous-language coverage 1.00. Feasibility remains risky due fail-closed parser-scope requirement. DECIDE 2026-07-13: build. Ready epics split by rollout boundary: EPIC-PLDD-ENFORCEMENT-KERNEL (Phase A, src-only, zero new dependencies) then EPIC-PLDD-CONSUMER-ADAPTERS (Phase B, src-only, inactive fixture-tested adapters). Phase B depends on Phase A. Dogfood activation is excluded and still requires separate human approval. SYNC 2026-07-13: added Phase A and Phase B epics to Iter4; dependency Phase B -> Phase A; dogfood activation excluded.
